{{Quote|The ''Force'' is with you, young Skywalker... but you're not a ''Jedi'' yet!|[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]|<ref name="Star Wars (1977) 44">[[Star Wars (1977) 44|''Star Wars'' (1977) 44]]</ref>}}
'''''Star Wars 44: The Empire Strikes Back: Duel a Dark Lord''''' is the forty-fourth issue in the [[Star Wars (1977)|''Marvel Star Wars'']] series of comics.

==Plot summary==
[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] arrives in [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and they begin looking for their friends. [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] delivers the frozen body of [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] over to [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] and instructs the [[Ugnaught/Legends|Ugnaught]] workers to reset the [[Carbon-freezing/Legends|carbon-freezing]] chamber for Skywalker. He then instructs his officers to have [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] brought aboard his ship. [[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]] fires back, claiming that part of the deal was that they would remain in the city. Vader venomously tells him that he is altering the deal.

The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] soldiers begin escorting Leia and Chewbacca back to the ship when they come upon Luke. A firefight takes place and the group splits up. Lando, feeling guilty over betraying Han, has his own men overtake the [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]. He frees Leia and Chewbacca and the partially re-constructed [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]]. They try to make it back to the landing pad to free Han, but they are too late. As they arrive, Boba Fett takes off with Han's body en route to his employer, [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba the Hutt]]. They board the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] and leave Cloud City.

Meanwhile, Luke goes to the carbon freezing facility where he finds Darth Vader waiting for him. They ignite their [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]] and begin dueling. Their fight takes them to a gantry hanging above one of the city's exhaust shafts. Vader slices off Luke's right hand with his saber. Luke accuses Vader of killing his father, to which Vader replies, "No, Luke. I am your father". Unable to handle the truth of the matter, Luke flings himself off the gantry where he begins falling to his death.

Falling through the various tunnel shafts below the city, he is eventually expelled and left hanging from a sensor vane on the underbelly of the floating city. He reaches out with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to project his thoughts to Leia. Leia, hearing Luke's telepathic cry for help, orders Lando to turn the Falcon around and go back for him. They pick Luke up and rendezvous with the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Rebel Fleet]].

Onboard a medical ship, Luke is cleaned up and his hand is replaced with a [[Cyborg/Legends|cybernetic]] replacement. Now he has to deal with his failure to rescue Han as well as the revelation that Darth Vader, the most evil being in the galaxy, is his father.

==Continuity==
This is Chapter Six of the six-part adaptation of [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']].

Luke Skywalker has two lightsabers on the cover: one in his hands and one on his belt.

Luke falls from the antenna while being rescued and then the ''Falcon'' catches him. In the film he doesn't fall while being rescued.
